_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Pasco/Hernando/Sumter batch (HD 52-56): samantha-scott, jeff-holcomb, randy-maggard, kevin-m-steele, brad-yeager. Evidence-first, every TRUE cited; null where no evidence. Currency catches: Scott (HD-52 is Sumter/Hernando, NOT Pasco) is a freshman seated March 2026 unopposed -> no voting record; Steele (HD-55) filed HD-55 re-election then entered the GOP primary for U.S. House CD-14 (running for higher office).
_meta.rule_calibration: SB300 6-week-ban YES -> sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE (pro-life affirmation); abolition q1 left null (a restriction is not abolition). HB543/HB133/HB759 -> self_defense q0/q1. SB1718 -> border q2 (anti-sanctuary)+q3 (E-Verify). HB1 -> family q0 only for confirmed cosponsors. HB1557/HB173 -> family q1/q2 + marriage q2/q4. HB95 armed church security -> christian_liberty q3. NRA-PVF/FOP/sheriff endorsements -> hard evidence on their issue cell.

profile.background: Director of Marketing for the family business (Mike Scott Plumbing); real estate; DeSantis-appointed Trustee, Lake-Sumter State College Board (2025); former Republican Party of Florida Moms Coalition chair. Born 1981, Orlando; resides Bushnell. HD-52 = Sumter (part) + Hernando County (north-central FL, NOT Pasco/Tampa Bay).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Freshman seated ~March 24-25 2026 via special election (predecessor John Temple resigned to lead Lake-Sumter State College); ran unopposed. Seated AFTER the 2026 regular session closed -> NO floor votes and NO sponsored bills exist. Scored only from the one cited endorsement (Florida FOP); everything else NO EVIDENCE -> null. Revisit after the 2027 session.

samantha-scott@FL.notes_set: Baptist R, HD-52 (Sumter/Hernando Co. -- north-central FL, NOT Pasco), seated ~March 2026 via unopposed special election after John Temple's resignation. Freshman with ZERO roll-call record (absent from all 2023 marquee votes -- SB300 6-week ban, HB543 permitless carry, HB1 vouchers -- and seated after the 2026 session ended). Only cited issue-evidence: endorsed by the Florida Fraternal Order of Police (self-described 'proud mother of a first responder') -> public_justice q0. Donors are GOP-establishment business groups (Associated Industries of FL, Americans for Prosperity); no Florida Right to Life / Florida Family Policy Council / NRA endorsement found. All other categories (life, marriage, family, Christian liberty, economic, elections, federalism, immigration, 2A) NO EVIDENCE -> null (not inferred from party). Seeking re-election in 2026; Democrat Pamala Bivins filed (primary Aug 18, general Nov 3 2026).

0.note: Endorsed by the Florida Fraternal Order of Police; self-described 'proud mother of a first responder' -- publicly backs law enforcement.

profile.background: Realtor; U.S. Navy Reserve intelligence officer (Lieutenant, 2006-present; Operation Inherent Resolve; Navy Commendation Medal). BA Economics (UMBC), MA Intelligence Studies (American Military Univ.). In office since Nov 2022 (re-elected 2024). HD-53 = Hernando/Pasco County.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 from HARD votes/sponsorships (FL House/Senate bill records, Freedom Index 81% lifetime / 100% 2025-26) and cited campaign statements. Christian (denomination not public). Note: HB1 individual floor vote NOT independently confirmed (Freedom Index lists it by its own inverted scoring) -> family q0 left null. HCR 693 Article-V NO is his only off-script recorded vote (ambiguous; not mapped).

jeff-holcomb@FL.notes_set: Christian R, HD-53 (Hernando/Pasco), Navy Reserve intelligence officer, in office since 2022. HARD record: voted YES on SB300 (2023 six-week abortion ban); co-introduced HB543 (2023 permitless carry) and HB1521 (2023 facility/sex-at-birth 'bathroom' bill); voted YES to lower the firearm-purchase age (HB1543/HB1223/HB759, rolling back the post-Parkland 21 limit); voted YES on 2A Financial Privacy (SB214) and 'Constitutional Money' (HB999); voted YES on anti-climate-mandate bills (HB1645, SB56); voted YES on HB601 (2024, banning anti-police civilian review boards) with FOP + Sheriff Nienhuis endorsements; campaign touts voting for FL's immigration-enforcement laws and removing DEI from schools. DEVIATION/notes: voted NO on HCR 693 (Article V term-limits convention, 2024) -- ambiguous, not mapped to a cell. No abortion-industry money or contrary (pro-left) position found. Christian Liberty + most Election-Integrity specifics = NO EVIDENCE -> null.

0.note: Voted YES on SB300, the 2023 six-week abortion ban -- documented pro-life vote (a restriction, not abolition; q1 left null).

2.note: Co-introduced HB1521 (2023) defining sex at birth for facilities; campaign supports banning transgender procedures for minors -- affirms immutable biological sex.

4.note: Campaign platform: remove DEI from schools and restrict drag performances for children -- opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in schools.

2.note: Campaign platform to remove DEI from K-12 plus HB1521 record -- opposes SOGI/gender-ideology curricula.

0.note: Voted YES on the 2A Financial Privacy Act (SB214) and 'Constitutional Money' (HB999, 2025) -- opposes CBDC, supports cash/sound money.

4.note: Voted YES on anti-'climate change' HB1645 and SB56 (prohibiting UN-backed climate intervention) -- anti-ESG/WEF capture posture.

0.note: Voted YES on HB601 (2024, banning anti-police civilian review boards); FOP and Sheriff Nienhuis endorsements -- publicly backs law enforcement.

2.note: Campaign: voted for FL's 'toughest immigration laws' (SB1718, 2023 session he was seated for) -- anti-sanctuary enforcement.

3.note: SB1718 (2023) expanded mandatory E-Verify for employment; he campaigns on having supported it.

1.note: Voted YES to lower the firearm-purchase age (HB1543/HB1223/HB759), rolling back the post-Parkland 21 limit -- opposes new gun restrictions.

profile.background: Full name Randall Scott 'Randy' Maggard. Born July 4 1963, Dade City; A.A. Pasco-Hernando State College. VP of a discount-appliance chain / small-business owner; ex-Chair Republican Party of Pasco (2006-12). Deputy Majority Leader / Chief Whip. In office since a 2019 special election (then HD-38; HD-54 after 2022 redistricting). HD-54 = Pasco County (Dade City).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Scored from HARD individual votes/cosponsorships (FL House/Senate bill records) and the party-line marquee SB300 (he is GOP Chief Whip in the supermajority). Religion listed generically 'Christian' (no named church). Categories with no documented position -> null.

randy-maggard@FL.notes_set: Christian R, HD-54 (Pasco/Dade City), GOP Deputy Majority Leader / Chief Whip, in office since 2019. HARD record: individual recorded YES on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary + E-Verify, House passed 83-36); cosponsored HB543 (2023 permitless carry), HB1 (2023 universal school-choice vouchers), and HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education). SB300 (2023 six-week abortion ban) passed the House 70-40 on party lines in his GOP supermajority -> recorded YES side (party-line marquee = hard). No documented cross-party deviation on any RESOLUTE category. Christian Liberty, economic, elections, public-justice, federalism = NO EVIDENCE -> null. Watch-item (non-category): filed a 2026 bill barring local water-pollution regulation, criticized by environmental press. Seeking re-election 2026 (endorsed by Speaker-designate Sam Garrison, Rep. Bilirakis, Sen. Burgess, AgCommr Simpson).

0.note: SB300 (2023 six-week abortion ban) passed the House 70-40 on party lines; as GOP Chief Whip he is on the recorded YES side -- documented pro-life vote.

4.note: Cosponsored HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education) -- opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity/SOGI content in public schools.

0.note: Cosponsored HB1 (2023), Florida's universal school-choice voucher expansion.

1.note: Cosponsored HB1557 (Parental Rights in Education) -- parental notification/consent framework on minors.

2.note: Cosponsored HB1557 -- restricts SOGI/gender-ideology instruction in K-12.
Individual recorded YES on SB1718 (2023) -- anti-sanctuary / federal-enforcement cooperation.

3.note: Individual recorded YES on SB1718, which expanded mandatory E-Verify for employment.

profile.background: Retired CEO; B.S. Business Management (Univ. of South Florida); married, four children; resides Dade City. In office since Nov 2022 (re-elected 2024). HD-55 = Pasco County. Committees: IT Budget & Policy (Vice Chair), Judiciary, Criminal Justice Subcommittee, Select Committee on Property Taxes.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. CANDIDACY CURRENCY: filed for CFO (Nov 2025), dropped that and announced HD-55 re-election (Feb 2026), then entered the GOP primary for U.S. House CD-14 vs. Kathy Castor (May 2026) -> running for higher office; HD-55 successors (Walter Price, Brenton Basinger) are already lining up. Still the seated HD-55 rep now. Individual 2023 marquee roll calls (SB300/HB543/HB1) were not retrievable -> those cells null; scored only HARD/strong-cited items.

kevin-m-steele@FL.notes_set: Catholic R, HD-55 (Pasco/Dade City), retired CEO, in office since 2022. CANDIDACY: filed CFO (Nov 2025) -> dropped for HD-55 re-election (Feb 2026) -> entered the GOP primary for U.S. House CD-14 challenging Kathy Castor (May 2026); running for higher office while still seated in HD-55. HARD/strong-cited record: prime sponsor of HB621 (2024 anti-squatter property-rights law, signed by DeSantis); sponsored HB113 (2025, Charlie Kirk roadway naming); endorsed by Sheriffs Chad Chronister, Chris Nocco, and Grady Judd with an 'always back the blue' brand (Criminal Justice + Judiciary committees) -> public_justice; NRA-endorsed 2022 candidate -> self_defense; CD-14 campaign: 'stand with President Trump to deport illegal aliens' -> immigration. No individual recorded votes on SB300/HB543/HB1 retrievable and he declined the iVoterGuide questionnaire, so life/marriage/family/economic/elections/federalism = NO EVIDENCE -> null. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Endorsed by Sheriffs Chad Chronister (Hillsborough), Chris Nocco (Pasco), and Grady Judd (Polk); 'always back the blue' brand; sits on the Criminal Justice Subcommittee -- publicly backs law enforcement.

1.note: Campaign statement: 'stand with President Trump to deport illegal aliens' -- supports mandatory deportation.

0.note: NRA-endorsed as a 2022 House candidate; pro-2A campaign language ('not the Second Amendment') -- supports the right to carry (FL is a permitless-carry state).

profile.background: Full name Bradford Troy 'Brad' Yeager. General Manager in the automotive industry (vehicle auctions) / small-business owner; Charlotte HS (Punta Gorda) 1994; wife Lisa. In office since Nov 2022 (re-elected 2024). HD-56 = Pasco County (New Port Richey / West Pasco).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 from HARD votes/sponsorships (FL House/Senate bill records incl. an individual SB300 roll-call line) and DeSantis endorsement. Christian (roster; no named church). School-choice (HB1) and 2025 immigration package support were caucus-inferred without an isolated individual vote -> left null. No documented contrary position on any category.

brad-yeager@FL.notes_set: Christian R, HD-56 (Pasco/New Port Richey), automotive GM, in office since 2022; DeSantis-endorsed 'conservative fighter'. HARD record: individual recorded YEA on SB300 (2023 six-week abortion ban, roll-call 'Y Yeager-56'); co-sponsored HB543 (2023 permitless carry) and HB133 (2026, lowering long-gun purchase age 21->18, repealing a post-Parkland limit; House passed 74-37); prime sponsor of HB173 (2026 Parental Rights -- parental consent on minor medical care and gender-identity surveys) and HB95 (2026, exempting volunteer armed church security from private-security licensing); sponsored HB125 (Charlie Kirk Day) and had a pastor open a House floor session in prayer. NO EVIDENCE (null): same-sex-marriage stance, euthanasia/IVF, election-integrity specifics, economic stewardship, public-justice specifics; HB1 school choice and the 2025 immigration package were caucus-inferred only -> null. Seeking re-election 2026 (faces Jessica Naioti in the Aug 18 GOP primary).

0.note: Individual recorded YEA on SB300, the 2023 six-week abortion ban (roll call 'Y Yeager-56') -- documented pro-life vote.

2.note: Prime sponsor of HB173 (2026), requiring parental consent on minor gender-identity surveys/medical care -- affirms immutable biological sex / rejects transgender ideology for minors.

4.note: HB173 restricts gender-identity surveys for minors without parental consent -- opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in schools.

1.note: Prime sponsor of HB173 (2026) -- parental notification/consent on minor medical, mental-health, and gender interventions.

2.note: HB173 limits gender-identity surveys/instruction for minors -- opposes SOGI/gender-ideology curricula in K-12.

3.note: Prime sponsor of HB95 (2026), exempting volunteer armed church security from private-security licensing -- protects houses of worship in the public square.

1.note: Co-sponsored HB133 (2026) lowering the long-gun purchase age 21->18, repealing a post-Parkland restriction -- opposes gun restrictions.
